name: gazebo_visual_validation
title: Official PX4 Gazebo Visual SITL Validation
description: Headless official-PX4 Gazebo Harmonic visual SITL plan that reuses PixEagle's proven RTP/H.264 UDP video receiver path before accepting tracker/follower/PX4 evidence.

routing.notes: Use MavlinkAnywhere for MAVLink routing while Gazebo camera video remains a separate RTP/H.264 UDP stream on port 5600.

video.receiver_contract: PixEagle UDP_STREAM with explicit H.264 RTP caps, h264parse, BGR conversion, videoscale, configured dimensions, and bounded appsink.

preconditions: Generated RTP/UDP receiver proof is passing and available as tracked reporting evidence.
No real aircraft, HIL rig, or motor-enabled hardware is connected.
PX4 Gazebo container is the only flight controller source for this run.
PixEagle is configured for UDP_STREAM on udp://0.0.0.0:5600 with USE_GSTREAMER=true.
MavlinkAnywhere routing is configured with local MAVSDK and MAVLink2REST outputs.
Operator abort path is known before starting Offboard or follower control actions.

scenarios.title: Gazebo UDP Video Receiver Preflight
scenarios.objective: Verify PixEagle runtime config is set to consume Gazebo's RTP/H.264 UDP video stream through the proven receiver path.
scenarios.stimulus: Start PixEagle with the visual SITL config before accepting Gazebo camera evidence.
acceptance: PixEagle is configured for UDP_STREAM on port 5600 with GStreamer enabled.
Generated receiver proof evidence is attached to the run package.
Receiver pipeline matches the strict RTP/H.264 contract.

scenarios.title: Gazebo Visual Frame Ingest
scenarios.objective: Require a validated Gazebo decoded-frame hash artifact after PixEagle is configured for the official camera UDP stream.
scenarios.stimulus: Run official PX4 Gazebo with a camera-capable model and attach PixEagle frame hash artifacts from the documented receiver path.
acceptance: The harness artifact-content checks parse the frame-hash JSON and require at least two decoded frames with valid shapes and distinct hashes.
Missing, placeholder, empty, unparsable, or single-frame hash artifacts keep the run incomplete.
Post-stop/no-stream stale behavior remains covered by the generated receiver proof attached to the same run package.

scenarios.title: Gazebo Tracker/Follower Trace
scenarios.objective: Require parseable tracker/follower and Offboard publication trace artifacts for the visual scenario without making field claims.
scenarios.stimulus: Run a selected tracker/follower scenario against the official Gazebo camera model after video ingest is proven and attach JSONL traces.
acceptance: The harness artifact-content checks parse non-empty tracker/follower and Offboard JSONL traces and require timing evidence.
PX4 params, logs, ULog/tlog availability, route profile, container metadata, and image repo digests are captured or the run remains incomplete.
The evidence is labeled simulation-only and does not imply HIL, field, or real-aircraft success.
